name: performance
description: ESP32 MicroPython operation costs — time and memory. Use these when deciding: can this run in an ISR? will this fit in memory? is this fast enough for my loop?
measurement_note: Approximate values. Actual times vary with CPU freq, WiFi activity, and concurrent tasks. WiFi-on adds ~15% overhead to all operations.

gpio.description: Pin operations are extremely fast. Safe for ISRs and high-frequency loops.

pwm.description: PWM is hardware-driven — no CPU overhead after setup. Safe for continuous use.

i2c.description: I2C is slow and blocking. Do NOT use in ISRs. SoftI2C is ~30% slower than hardware I2C.

spi.description: SPI is fast but blocking. Hardware SPI preferred over SoftSPI (4x faster).

adc.description: ADC is moderately slow. ESP32 ADC is nonlinear near 0V and 3.3V — best range is 0.2V–3.1V.

ops.name: ADC.read_uv()
ops.notes: Calibrated microvolt reading — use for accurate values

wifi.description: WiFi operations are SLOW and MEMORY-HEAVY. Never in ISRs. Never in tight loops.

ops.name: wlan.scan()
ops.notes: Blocks for 2 seconds. Returns list of SSIDs.

ops.name: wlan.connect(ssid, pwd)
ops.notes: Blocks up to 15s. Use polling pattern.

socket_http.description: Network operations are the most expensive. Close sockets immediately.

memory.description: Memory allocation costs. Avoid allocation in loops.

filesystem.description: Flash operations are slow and wear-limited. Buffer writes.

ops.name: write 1KB to file
ops.notes: Flash write — slow, wears flash (~100K cycles per sector)

rules_of_thumb.isr_safe: Only GPIO read/write and ticks_ms() are ISR-safe. Nothing else.
rules_of_thumb.tight_loop: At 1000Hz loop: keep per-iteration work under 800µs. Use readinto() instead of read().
rules_of_thumb.battery_life: WiFi active: ~100mA. WiFi off + light sleep: ~5mA. Deep sleep: ~5µA. Every second of WiFi costs 20,000x more power than deep sleep.
rules_of_thumb.flash_wear: Flash sector: ~100K erase cycles. Writing config every second wears flash in ~27 hours. Write config max once per minute.
rules_of_thumb.heap_fragmentation: Long-running apps should avoid creating/destroying objects of different sizes. Pre-allocate fixed-size bytearrays.
